Coc-c..Bladder: Urging to urinate, but inability to pass urine until a large clot of blood has been passed.
Coc-c..Generalities: The patient is cold, and his complaints come on in cold weather. He is sensitive to cold, easily takes cold. But you must distinguish between the patient himself and his complaints, because they are entirely opposite to each other. When he once becomes sick from exposure to cold, he is always worse in a warm room and better in the cold air.
Coc-c..Genitalia female: Uterine hemorrhage is a strong feature of this remedy. Copious, frequent, prolonged menstrual flow. Large, hard, black clots fill the uterus, are expelled by labor-like pains, and form again. Inflammation of uterus and vagina, with copious, thick, white, jelly-like, ropy mucus. Soreness of the vulva; cannot bear the pressure of the clothing.
Coc-c..Generalities, heat, sensation of: There is a general hyperesthesia of the skin and mucous membranes. Sensitive to the pressure of the clothing.
Coc-c..Throat: Sore throat with redness. Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx. Fauces very sensitive. Arch of palate and fauces, as far as visible, very red. Burning in the throat < in the warmth, especially when warmed up in bed, < from warm drinks, though hot drinks are not so bad. Better from cold drinks.
Coc-c..Generalities, heat: His cough is brought on in a warm room; from being too warm in bed; from drinking warm things. It is better from drinking cold things in a cold room; worse from exertion; from getting heated up; from becoming warm; that is, after the complaint has once set in, it reverses itself.
Coc-c..Genitalia female, menses: Copious, frequent, prolonged menstrual flow. Large, hard, black clots fill the uterus, are expelled by labor-like pains, and form again.
Coc-c..Larynx and trachea: If the patient gets warm in bed or the room gets warm he commences to clutch the larynx and cough. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
Coc-c..Urine: Albumen in the urine. Dark red sediment in the urine. Pain shooting from the kidney to the bladder and down the legs; < from motion.
Coc-c..Cough: Spasmodic cough; whooping cough; the cough of drunkards. The chronic catarrhal state of the Coccus cacti patient comes on especially in the winter. It comes on when the cold weather begins and lasts till the warm weather comes.
Coc-c..Expectoration: Finally he retches and vomits long strings of tough, ropy mucus filling the mouth and throat and causing him to choke. This is due to the fact that the mucus is so tenacious that it cannot be ejected from the pharynx in the usual way, therefore he must vomit.
Coc-c..Cough, tickling: Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
Coc-c..Expectoration, viscid: Great quantities of this mucus form in the nose, in the throat, in the air passages generally, and in the vagina. Finally he retches and vomits long strings of tough, ropy mucus filling the mouth and throat and causing him to choke.
Coc-c..Larynx and trachea, irritation, larynx: If the patient gets warm in bed or the room gets warm he commences to clutch the larynx and cough. The slightest touch on the palate or even the gums in examination of the throat will cause gagging sometimes when the parts look normal.
Coc-c..Larynx and trachea, tickling in the air: Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
Coc-c..Nose: Discharge of thick yellow mucus from the nose; nose stopped up, with inclination to sneeze. Great dryness of the nose.
Coc-c..Larynx and trachea, irritation: Anything coming in contact with the pharynx, the inside of the mouth, or even the gums, produces gagging and retching and will bring on the cough.
Coc-c..Larynx and trachea, tickling: Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
Coc-c..Larynx and trachea, larynx in general: If the patient gets warm in bed or the room gets warm he commences to clutch the larynx and cough. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
Coc-c..Bladder, urging to urinate (morbid desir: Urging to urinate, but inability to pass urine until a large clot of blood has been passed.
Urging to urinate, but inability to pass urine until a large clot of blood has been passed.
Coc-c..Cough, hacking, tickling in larynx, from: Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx. He cannot hawk without gagging.
Coc-c..Cough, irritation in air passages, from: Anything coming in contact with the pharynx, the inside of the mouth, or even the gums, produces gagging and retching and will bring on the cough.
Coc-c..Cough, irritation in air passages, from,: If the patient gets warm in bed or the room gets warm he commences to clutch the larynx and cough.
Coc-c..Cough, night: They are apt to be worse at night when he becomes warm in bed. If he can lie in a cool room without much covering he will go longer without coughing.
Coc-c..Cough, paroxysmal: It is a spasmodic cough, forcing the patient into the most violent efforts. The face becomes purple. Finally he retches and vomits long strings of tough, ropy mucus filling the mouth and throat and causing him to choke.
